message:
fix(time_series): percentile(NaN) returns None instead of the smallest sample (ft-jz40p)

[MEDIUM] Same class as ft-b4l62 (telemetry Histogram::quantile) in a
second file. `TimeSeries::percentile_range` relied on `p.clamp(0.0,
1.0)` to validate its input, but `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _)` returns NaN
(documented gotcha). The NaN cascades through:

  let p = p.clamp(0.0, 1.0);                    // NaN stays NaN
  let idx = ((values.len() - 1) as f64 * p)     // NaN
      .round() as usize;                        // NaN as usize = 0
  Some(values[idx])                             // smallest sample

A NaN quantile arg silently returned the smallest value — a
plausible-looking wrong answer. `percentile()` delegates to the same
range function so both are fixed by a single entry guard.

## Fix

Fail closed on NaN at entry: `if values.is_empty() || p.is_nan() {
return None; }`. Matches the ft-b4l62 disposition so the two quantile
surfaces behave consistently.

## Tests (2 new)

* `ft_jz40p_percentile_nan_returns_none` — pins None return for NaN
  across both `percentile` and `percentile_range` surfaces.
* `ft_jz40p_percentile_infinity_inputs_still_work` — regression:
  ±infinity still clamp to 0.0/1.0 and return min/max samples,
  preserving the pre-existing contract.

## Verification

`cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features` on a
fresh `/tmp/ft-rv-p3-target` — lib compiles clean (189 pre-existing
warnings, no new ones).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
